speaker: Man
en: Good morning.

speaker: Man
en: Ken's Appliance.

speaker: Man
en: Can I help you?

speaker: Woman
en: Yes.

speaker: Woman
en: I'm ringing about my fridge.

speaker: Woman
en: It's not working at the moment.

speaker: Man
en: I'd like to find out a few more details, if I may.

speaker: Woman
en: Yes, sure.

speaker: Man
en: First I need to make sure if your fridge is still covered by warranty.

speaker: Woman
en: Oh, right.

speaker: Woman
en: I bought it just last week and it originally came with 2 years of warranty coverage, but I've extended the period to 3 years.

speaker: Woman
en: So it's definitely under warranty.

speaker: Man
en: Right.

speaker: Man
en: Now, what model is it?

speaker: Woman
en: Let me take a look … It should be somewhere on this warranty card.

speaker: Woman
en: Oh, here, on the top right-hand corner.

speaker: Woman
en: It's a Top Mount.

speaker: Man
en: And the colour of the fridge?

speaker: Woman
en: It's a silver one.

speaker: Woman
en: It is really modern and perfectly matches the whole décor of my kitchen.

speaker: Man
en: I've made a note of that.

speaker: Man
en: So can you tell me the exact date you purchased the fridge?

speaker: Woman
en: I remember it was delivered on January 15th.

speaker: Woman
en: I must have bought it a couple of days earlier.

speaker: Woman
en: Right, it was on the 12th.

speaker: Man
en: OK.

speaker: Man
en: Then what is the problem with the fridge?

speaker: Woman
en: Oh, it's a complete disaster.

speaker: Woman
en: First there is something wrong with the alarm.

speaker: Woman
en: I mean it is beeping continuously, even when the door is shut.

speaker: Woman
en: It's really annoying.

speaker: Woman
en: And there's also a problem with its temperature control.

speaker: Woman
en: It's now 10 degrees, but I set it to be minus 8.

speaker: Woman
en: It seemed to be working fine for the first couple of days.

speaker: Man
en: Sorry about that, but rest assured, Madam.

speaker: Man
en: We'll fix it all.

speaker: Woman
en: Where is your maintenance centre?

speaker: Man
en: Ken's Appliance is located on Elm Street, just a few steps from the station.

speaker: Man
en: And, did the broken fridge cause any loss to you?

speaker: Woman
en: Yes, actually it was used to store food for my sandwich shop.

speaker: Man
en: Any idea of the total value of loss?

speaker: Woman
en: Let me make an estimate here.

speaker: Woman
en: The vegetables in the fridge cost me around $50, and the meat cost more than $100.

speaker: Woman
en: So the overall loss is about $180.

speaker: Woman
en: I didn't notice the abnormal temperature until something inside went bad and the whole fridge smelled terrible.

speaker: Woman
en: I had to toss everything away.

speaker: Woman
en: Luckily, no one ate the food inside and got sick.

speaker: Man
en: That's a relief.

speaker: Woman
en: But the damage has really caused me great inconvenience.

speaker: Woman
en: I've nowhere to put the raw materials and thus could not open for business these days.

speaker: Woman
en: The longer it takes to fix the fridge, the more it will cost me.

speaker: Woman
en: You know … I have a whole bunch of bills to pay, like water, electricity, rent …

speaker: Man
en: Don't worry, we'll attend to your problems as soon as possible.

speaker: Woman
en: I need to know precisely when you can send a guy to fix all the problems.

speaker: Woman
en: It is very urgent.

speaker: Woman
en: Is there anyone who can keep me posted on the latest information?

speaker: Man
en: How about this?

speaker: Man
en: I'll have the manager ring you later.

speaker: Man
en: Does that work for you?

speaker: Woman
en: OK … I'll be expecting his call then.

speaker: Woman
en: Do you know what exactly is wrong with the fridge?

speaker: Man
en: For your case, we assume it's the door that has to be fixed.

speaker: Man
en: The abnormal temperature and the noisy alarm both indicate that.

speaker: Woman
en: Yes, indeed.

speaker: Woman
en: I've just noticed that the door is dented.

speaker: Man
en: It may have to be replaced then.

speaker: Man
en: But we'll have to check the availability of our repair guy first.

speaker: Man
en: We'll phone you back later.

speaker: Woman
en: OK, thank you.

speaker: Man
en: You're welcome.

speaker: Man
en: Bye.
